failure_signature.details: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'langchain_community'. The 'langchain-community' package is required but not installed.

problem.goal_state: Import LangChain community tools for agent setup

solutions.solution_summary: Install the missing langchain-community package and verify the package ecosystem
solution_steps: Install the package: pip install langchain-community
Since LangChain 0.2+, community integrations were split into separate packages. Also install provider-specific packages as needed: pip install langchain-openai langchain-anthropic
Verify the installation: python -c 'import langchain_community; print(langchain_community.__version__)'
Check for version compatibility: pip install 'langchain-community>=0.3.0,<0.4.0' to match your langchain version
Update all langchain packages together to avoid version mismatches: pip install --upgrade langchain langchain-core langchain-community

failure_signature.details: ERROR: pip's dependency resolver found conflicting requirements: crewai 0.80.4 requires pydantic>=2.4,<3.0 but autogen-agentchat 0.4.7 requires pydantic>=1.10,<2.0. Cannot install both packages.

problem.goal_state: Use both CrewAI and AutoGen in the same Python project

solutions.solution_summary: Isolate conflicting frameworks in separate virtual environments or use compatible versions
solution_steps: Check if a newer version of autogen supports Pydantic v2: pip install 'autogen-agentchat>=0.4.7' may resolve the conflict
If no compatible versions exist, run each framework in a separate virtual environment and communicate via an API or message queue
Use Docker containers to isolate each framework: one container for CrewAI, another for AutoGen, connected via HTTP or gRPC
Check the autogen migration guide for Pydantic v2 support: newer versions of autogen (0.4.x) have moved to Pydantic v2
As a temporary workaround, pin pydantic to v2 and test if autogen works with compatibility shims: pip install 'pydantic>=2.4' and test thoroughly

failure_signature.details: ImportError: cannot import name 'ChatOpenAI' from 'langchain.chat_models'. This class has been moved to 'langchain_openai'. Please update your import.

problem.goal_state: Migrate LangChain project from v0.1 to v0.3 import paths

solutions.solution_summary: Update import paths to match the LangChain v0.3 package structure
solution_steps: Replace 'from langchain.chat_models import ChatOpenAI' with 'from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI'
Install the provider package: pip install langchain-openai
Use the LangChain migration CLI tool to automate import updates: langchain-cli migrate --diff to preview changes, then langchain-cli migrate to apply
Common import changes: langchain.embeddings.OpenAIEmbeddings -> langchain_openai.OpenAIEmbeddings, langchain.llms.Anthropic -> langchain_anthropic.ChatAnthropic
Check the LangChain migration guide at python.langchain.com/docs/versions/migrating_chains for a complete mapping of old to new imports

failure_signature.details: RuntimeError: Failed to load chromadb native extensions. The installed binary was built for glibc 2.35 but this system has glibc 2.31. Rebuild from source or use a compatible binary.

problem.goal_state: Set up ChromaDB vector store for AutoGen retrieval-augmented agents

solutions.solution_summary: Install a compatible binary or rebuild from source for the target platform
solution_steps: Check your glibc version: ldd --version
Try installing an older version of chromadb that was built for your glibc: pip install 'chromadb==0.4.24'
Build from source: pip install chromadb --no-binary chromadb (requires build tools: apt install build-essential)
Alternatively, use a Docker container based on a newer Ubuntu (22.04+) that includes glibc 2.35+
Consider using an alternative vector store that does not require native extensions: pip install qdrant-client for Qdrant or use FAISS with faiss-cpu

failure_signature.details: ImportError: To use the OpenAI Agents SDK, you need the 'openai-agents' package. Install it with: pip install openai-agents[all]

problem.goal_state: Set up OpenAI Agents SDK with all optional features

solutions.solution_summary: Install the package with all optional dependencies using extras syntax
solution_steps: Install with all optional dependencies: pip install 'openai-agents[all]'
For minimal installation, install only what you need: pip install openai-agents for core, pip install 'openai-agents[viz]' for visualization
Verify the installation: python -c 'from agents import Agent; print("OK")'
If using a requirements.txt, add the extras: openai-agents[all]>=1.0.0,<2.0.0
Ensure Python 3.10+ is being used as the SDK requires modern Python features
